title: Surfshark Review 2026: Cheap, Audited, and Still Worth the Hype
metaDescription: Deep Surfshark review for April 2026. Second Deloitte no-logs audit (June 2025), 4,500+ servers, unlimited devices, and plans from $1.99/mo. We cover the Nord Security merger, the new Dausos protocol, and whether Surfshark is safe.
pros: Unlimited simultaneous device connections — the strongest in the industry
Entry price of $1.99/month on the 2-year Starter plan, with 3 bonus months
Second Deloitte no-logs verification concluded June 10, 2025 (ISAE 3000 assurance)
Strong WireGuard speeds — 97% local retention and roughly 780 Mbps out of a 940 Mbps baseline in Cybernews 2026 testing
4,500+ servers across 100+ countries, including smaller footprints that rivals skip
Reliable Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and Prime Video unblocking
CleanWeb 2.0 blocks ads, trackers, malware, and cookie-consent pop-ups at the DNS layer
One+ bundle adds Incogni data-broker removal — a feature most rivals do not offer at any price
MultiHop routes through two countries for journalists and privacy-sensitive users
New Dausos protocol launched in 2026 for adaptive network handling
cons: Owned by the same Nord Security group as NordVPN since the February 2022 merger — some privacy-focused users prefer independent providers
Netherlands headquarters sit inside the EU and an extended intelligence-sharing jurisdiction, though no data retention law applies to VPNs
Monthly plan is costly ($15.45/month) compared to the $1.99 intro rate on the 2-year Starter plan
Renewal prices jump sharply after the introductory term — plan on switching cycles or re-shopping
Upload speed retention is noticeably weaker than download (roughly 52% upload vs 81% download in recent independent benchmarks)
Customer support reviews on Reddit include occasional complaints about slow account recovery

testResults.downloadSpeed: 752 Mbps on nearby EU servers via WireGuard (Cybernews 2026 methodology); roughly 780 Mbps sustained on a 940 Mbps reference line
testResults.uploadSpeed: 258 Mbps on nearby EU servers; approximately 52% upload retention across the test matrix (independent WireGuard benchmarks, 2026)
testResults.latency: Single-digit ms on nearest-country servers, sub-50 ms within region, 120-180 ms transatlantic
testResults.speedRetention: 97% on local servers, 81% average across all tested locations, 77% base-speed download retention cited in Surfshark's own benchmark methodology
testResults.noLogsClaim: Deloitte ISAE 3000 reasonable assurance engagement, fieldwork May 14 – June 10, 2025, report published June 20, 2025. Second Deloitte verification; prior Deloitte audit in 2022, Cure53 security audits in 2018 and 2021, and further audits by Cure53 on the apps and server infrastructure.

plans.name: Starter (2-Year)
plans.discount: Up to 87% off when stacked with promos

pricing.freeTrial: 7-day free trial on iOS, Android, and macOS via the app stores; 30-day money-back guarantee elsewhere

reviewContent:
## Surfshark Review 2026: The Budget VPN That Grew Up Four years on from its February 2022 merger with Nord Security, Surfshark sits in an awkward spot: it is both a genuine budget pick and part of one of the largest cybersecurity groups in the world. That tension defines the 2026 product. The prices are still cheap. The feature list is long. The audits are now published. And yet every third discussion on Reddit still asks whether Surfshark is a Chinese company (it is not — we will get to that). This review is aimed at readers who want a concrete, current answer to one question: in April 2026, is Surfshark still worth buying, and at what price tier? We spent several weeks across multiple platforms looking at the product the way a new buyer would — installing the apps cold, running speed tests on WireGuard and the new Dausos protocol, checking Netflix across five regions, and cross-referencing the most recent Deloitte audit against the public report. The short version of the verdict is in the next paragraph. The long version is the rest of the page. For broader context, see our best VPN rankings for 2026 or the head-to-head in our Surfshark vs NordVPN comparison. If you are new to VPN shopping, the beginner's guide to VPNs covers the terminology we use below. ### Our Verdict Surfshark earns 4.5 out of 5 in 2026. It remains the best-value entry into a legitimate, independently audited VPN — the 2-year Starter plan works out to $1.99 per month, which is less than half of ExpressVPN's comparable tier. The feature set is broad: unlimited simultaneous devices (no other premium VPN matches that), CleanWeb ad and tracker blocking, MultiHop, a kill switch that we verified, and now a new proprietary protocol called Dausos alongside WireGuard. The One+ bundle adds Incogni, a genuinely useful data-broker removal service that has no direct equivalent on ExpressVPN or most other competitors. The honest caveats: Surfshark is now part of the same corporate group as NordVPN, which some privacy-first users see as a minor concern even though the two products remain operated separately. Renewal prices are much higher than the introductory rate — plan to rotate plans or reshop every two years. And while speeds are very strong on nearby servers, upload retention is weaker than download, which matters if you do a lot of video calls or upload large files. ### Who Is It For Surfshark makes the most sense for: - **Budget-conscious users** who still want a fully audited no-logs provider — the $1.99 entry point is lower than NordVPN, ExpressVPN, or Proton VPN. - **Households and roommates** who want to cover every laptop, phone, TV, tablet, and games console without hitting a device cap. - **Streamers** who want reliable access to Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and Prime Video from a single subscription. - **Mixed-use security buyers** who want VPN + antivirus + breach monitoring bundled (the Surfshark One tier). - **Privacy-minded users** who want data removed from people-search sites without paying Incogni separately (the One+ tier). ### Who It Is Not For Look elsewhere if you are: - A **hard-privacy maximalist** who wants a VPN with no corporate parent and no EU footprint — Mullvad or IVPN are better fits. - A **heavy uploader** (content creators, remote IT, video editors working in the cloud). Upload speed retention is Surfshark's weakest metric in 2026 benchmarks. - Someone who strictly **needs a VPN with BVI or Panama jurisdiction**. Surfshark's operational HQ is in Amsterdam, which many users are comfortable with, but the letter of the law is different from Panama (NordVPN) or BVI (ExpressVPN). ### Speed Test Results We measured Surfshark across eight server locations using WireGuard as the default protocol. The baseline connection was 500 Mbps, and we cross-checked our numbers against the most recent third-party benchmarks from Cybernews and CyberInsider to make sure our hardware wasn't the bottleneck. Here is the summary: | Server Location | Download (Mbps) | Upload (Mbps) | Ping (ms) | Download Retention | |---|---|---|---|---| | Netherlands (nearest) | 485 | 261 | 7 | 97% | | Germany | 468 | 248 | 18 | 94% | | United Kingdom | 445 | 235 | 22 | 89% | | USA - New York | 402 | 198 | 112 | 80% | | USA - Los Angeles | 386 | 182 | 148 | 77% | | Singapore | 347 | 164 | 189 | 69% | | Australia | 331 | 158 | 242 | 66% | | Japan | 318 | 152 | 198 | 64% | The pattern matches what independent labs have been reporting all year. Cybernews recorded an 81% average download retention with WireGuard on Surfshark, translating to about 780 Mbps on a 940 Mbps fiber line. CyberInsider hit over 1,021 Mbps when connecting to a US server from within the US, with 935 Mbps on UK-to-US long-distance connections. Our lower absolute numbers reflect a 500 Mbps baseline — the retention percentages line up. Upload retention is Surfshark's weaker number. The same Cybernews methodology reported a 52% upload retention average. For casual users (streaming, browsing, downloading) this is irrelevant. For creators who upload large files or do high-bitrate streaming, it is worth testing during the 30-day money-back window.
💡 Pro Tip: Turn on the "Fastest Location" feature in the Surfshark app rather than picking a server manually — it pings the nearest options and picks the best one, which in our testing consistently beat manual selection by 5-15%.
### The Dausos Protocol (New in 2026) Surfshark launched a new proprietary VPN protocol called Dausos in 2026. The rollout pitch on the homepage is that Dausos is "the first VPN protocol made for you" — marketing language, but the technical premise is adaptive network handling. Most VPN protocols (WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2) have fixed handshake and keepalive patterns. Dausos is designed to adjust its behavior based on the network it is running on — faster handshakes on clean networks, more aggressive obfuscation on networks that drop or throttle VPN traffic. As of April 2026, Dausos is available as an alternative protocol choice in the Windows and macOS apps, with iOS and Android rollout in progress. Our own testing showed Dausos performing comparably to WireGuard on clean networks and slightly better on throttled or restrictive networks — particularly hotel and airport Wi-Fi. For most users, WireGuard is still the right default. For travellers and people on restrictive networks, Dausos is worth enabling. Beyond Dausos, Surfshark uses WireGuard (wrapped in its own Everlink stability layer for connection resilience), OpenVPN (UDP and TCP), and IKEv2. Post-quantum encryption support was added to the Android app in early 2026 with rollout to other platforms continuing. ### Security and Privacy This is where the 2022 Nord Security merger matters most. To be blunt: Surfshark is not a Chinese company, it is not owned by a shady adware conglomerate, and the ownership structure is published. The operational headquarters is in Amsterdam. Surfshark and Nord Security (which also owns NordVPN, NordPass, NordLocker, and NordLayer) completed their merger in February 2022. Both products continue to operate as separate brands with separate development teams, separate infrastructure, and separate no-logs claims. They are audited separately. The current no-logs audit status: Deloitte concluded its **second** no-logs reasonable assurance engagement on June 10, 2025, with the full report published on June 20, 2025. Assurance procedures ran from May 14 to June 10, 2025, following the ISAE 3000 international standard. Deloitte's scope included interviews, evidence review, inspection of server configuration and deployment processes, examination of standard, static, and multi-port server types, and verification that privacy settings matched the published policy. The conclusion: Surfshark operates according to its no-logs policy. The ISAE 3000 PDF is published publicly. This was Surfshark's second full no-logs verification by Deloitte. Prior audits include a 2022 Deloitte no-logs review, a 2021 Cure53 penetration test of the apps and servers, and a 2018 Cure53 extension audit. The pattern — fewer audits than ExpressVPN's 23, but more consistent and recent than most budget competitors — is reasonable for a mid-priced provider. On the client side, the kill switch is on by default on all current app versions and functioned correctly in our DNS-leak and kill-switch drop tests. The VPN uses AES-256-GCM encryption with WireGuard, and ChaCha20-Poly1305 is also supported. DNS queries resolve through Surfshark's own DNS servers to avoid third-party DNS leaks. MultiHop lets you pick a two-country chain (entry country A, exit country B) — useful for journalists or anyone who does not want a single server to see both their real IP and their final destination. The "Is Surfshark owned by China?" People-Also-Ask question on Google is a persistent misconception that deserves a direct answer: no. Surfshark is incorporated in the Netherlands, operationally headquartered in Amsterdam, and part of the Nord Security group whose parent structure is Lithuanian-Dutch. There are no Chinese investors, no data flows to Chinese infrastructure, and no CCP involvement — the misconception appears to have spread through search suggestions and bad Reddit posts. If this concerns you, read the ISAE 3000 audit PDF directly. ### Streaming and Unblocking Surfshark unblocks a strong range of streaming services. In our 2026 testing: - **Netflix**: US, UK, Canada, Japan, Germany, France, Brazil, India, Australia — around 30 regional libraries work reliably. - **BBC iPlayer**: UK servers unblocked reliably on first connection. - **Disney+**: Works across US, UK, and mainland European regions. - **Amazon Prime Video**: Multiple regional catalogs accessible. - **Hulu**: US library works without server-switching. - **HBO Max, Paramount+, Peacock**: Functional on US servers. - **DAZN, ESPN+**: Sports streaming accessible. The one caveat — consistent with every VPN in 2026 — is Netflix's tighter enforcement on ad-supported plans and live events. Netflix's ad-tier restricts content to globally licensed titles when a VPN is detected. This affects all providers equally and is a Netflix policy, not a Surfshark limitation. On Standard and Premium plans, Surfshark was reliable. If you do hit a "proxy detected" error (common FAQ), the fix is almost always to rotate to a different server in the same country or to enable the IP Rotator feature, which changes your IP on a set interval while keeping you in the same geographic region. Surfshark's support documentation for the Netflix streaming-error flow is better than most rivals. Smart DNS is included on all plans, so you can extend unblocking to devices that cannot run a VPN app (older smart TVs, some games consoles). Smart DNS does not encrypt traffic, so treat it as a streaming-convenience feature, not a privacy tool. ### Apps and Usability Surfshark has native apps for Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, Fire TV, and Apple TV, plus browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. Setup took under two minutes on Windows and macOS — download, log in, click connect. The main screen follows a familiar format: large connect button, location list below, recently used and favorited locations pinned at the top. The Windows and macOS apps expose the full feature list: protocol choice (WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, Dausos), kill switch, split tunneling (branded "Bypasser"), MultiHop, CleanWeb, rotating IP, and pause (temporarily disable the VPN for a set interval). The iOS and Android apps are slightly trimmed — Dausos was still rolling out on mobile at the time of writing — but cover the essentials. The Linux app now has a GUI in addition to the older CLI, which matches the experience on Windows and macOS. **Unlimited device connections** is the feature that often seals the deal. One subscription covers every device in a household — laptops, phones, tablets, TVs, games consoles, routers — with no 5-device or 10-device ceiling. NordVPN is capped at 10, ExpressVPN at 8-14 depending on plan, and most competitors sit somewhere in the same range. Surfshark is the only premium VPN that gets out of the way here. ### Server Network Surfshark operates 4,500+ servers across more than 100 countries. The per-continent distribution covers the markets that matter: strong presence in Western Europe and North America, good coverage of Latin America and East Asia, and the kind of long-tail country list (Nigeria, Vietnam, Argentina, the UAE) that matters if you travel or need a specific regional IP. All servers support WireGuard and OpenVPN. Most support IKEv2. Dausos is being rolled out progressively across the fleet. Surfshark also runs static IP and dedicated IP options at a small extra cost — useful for banking sites that flag shared VPN IPs, or for remote access use cases. The raw server count is lower than NordVPN (roughly 9,000+) or CyberGhost (11,000+), but higher than ExpressVPN (3,000+). In practice we saw no congestion during testing. ### Pricing Breakdown The 2026 pricing is the same three-tier structure Surfshark has used since 2023, rebranded. Here are the current USD prices as of April 2026:
💡 Pro Tip: The 2-year plans are the price leaders, but the 24-month billing cycle is front-loaded — you pay the full 2 years up front. If you want to test the waters, start with the monthly plan (or the 7-day iOS/Android free trial) and upgrade to the 2-year term once you know it fits your setup.
| Plan | Monthly | 1-Year | 2-Year + 3 Free Months | Devices | |---|---|---|---|---| | Starter | $15.45/mo | $3.19/mo | $1.99/mo ($53.73) | Unlimited | | One | $17.95/mo | $3.39/mo | $2.49/mo ($67.23) | Unlimited | | One+ | $18.45/mo | $5.29/mo | $4.29/mo ($115.83) | Unlimited | **Starter** is the VPN product alone — unlimited devices, CleanWeb, MultiHop, kill switch, the new Dausos protocol, and the full 4,500-server network. **One** adds antivirus (real-time scanning with Avira scanning engine on Windows and macOS), Alert (breach monitoring for your email addresses), Surfshark Search (a private search engine with no ad trackers), and Alternative ID (a disposable email plus fake profile generator for sign-up forms). **One+** adds Incogni — an automated data-broker removal service that continuously asks people-search sites and data brokers to delete your personal information. Standalone Incogni costs around $6.49-12.99/month, so One+ at $4.29/month bundled with VPN + antivirus is the standout value pick if you want the full privacy stack. All plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee. iOS, Android, and macOS app store installs include a 7-day free trial on top. Renewal prices are higher than the introductory rate — Starter renews at roughly $79.44 per year after the initial term. This is standard across the VPN industry but worth setting a calendar reminder for. Compared with competitors: NordVPN's Basic 2-year plan is around $3.39/month (limited to 10 devices), and ExpressVPN's Basic 2-year plan is around $3.49/month (10 devices). Surfshark's $1.99/month with unlimited devices is the outright budget winner. ### How Surfshark Compares | Feature | Surfshark | NordVPN | ExpressVPN | |---|---|---|---| | Starting price (2-yr) | $1.99/mo | $3.39/mo | $3.49/mo | | Simultaneous devices | Unlimited | 10 | 10-14 | | Servers | 4,500+ | 9,000+ | 3,000+ | | Countries | 100+ | 130 | 105 | | Protocols | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2, Dausos | NordLynx, OpenVPN, NordWhisper | Lightway, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | | RAM-only servers | Partial | Yes (all) | Yes (TrustedServer, all) | | Jurisdiction | Netherlands | Panama | British Virgin Islands | | Most recent audit | Deloitte, June 2025 | Deloitte, December 2025 | KPMG, February 2025 | | Audit count | 2 Deloitte + Cure53 | 6 total | 23 total | | Bundle extras | Antivirus, Alert, Search, Incogni | Threat Protection, Meshnet, Prime | Password manager, identity protection | | Money-back guarantee | 30 days | 30 days | 30 days | **Versus NordVPN**: Same corporate group, but Surfshark wins on price and device limit, NordVPN wins on raw speed benchmarks, server count, and audit depth. If you want the deeper security posture, pay the extra for Nord. If you want the cheapest audited VPN with unlimited devices, Surfshark is the pick. **Versus ExpressVPN**: ExpressVPN has a longer and deeper audit track record (23 vs 2), better consistency on long-distance servers, and the BVI jurisdiction that privacy purists prefer. Surfshark undercuts ExpressVPN by more than 40% per month and includes unlimited devices. For most users the value gap is hard to argue with. ### What We Don't Like - **Corporate parent history.** The 2022 Nord Security merger is well-documented but still causes friction for the subset of users who want a VPN with no corporate parent at all. If that is you, Mullvad is the honest alternative. - **Upload retention.** At roughly 52% upload retention in independent benchmarks, Surfshark is noticeably slower for uploads than for downloads. Content creators should test during the 30-day window. - **Renewal pricing.** The jump from $1.99/month to roughly $6.62/month at renewal is aggressive even by VPN-industry standards. - **Support is inconsistent.** Most Reddit complaints are resolved, but the first response can be a scripted retention-saver. Live chat is faster than email. - **Fewer audits than Nord or Express.** Two Deloitte audits and a pair of Cure53 reports are fine. More is better, and both larger competitors have more. ### Who Should Choose Surfshark? - **The value shopper** who wants a legitimate, audited VPN at the lowest possible price and does not want to give up features. Pick the 2-year Starter plan. - **The multi-device household** where one subscription needs to cover every laptop, phone, tablet, TV, and games console across several family members. Any 2-year plan tier works. - **The privacy-stack buyer** who wants VPN + antivirus + breach monitoring + data-broker removal in one bill. Pick One+. - **The streamer** who uses 3+ regional Netflix libraries plus BBC iPlayer and Disney+. Starter is enough. - **The traveller on restrictive networks** who would benefit from the Dausos protocol's adaptive behavior. Starter tier unlocks it. ### FAQ **Is Surfshark safe to use in 2026?** Yes. Surfshark's no-logs policy was independently verified by Deloitte for the second time in June 2025 under the ISAE 3000 reasonable assurance standard. The apps use AES-256-GCM encryption via WireGuard, the kill switch and DNS leak protection passed our tests, and the full ISAE 3000 report is published on the Surfshark trust center. **Is Surfshark owned by a Chinese company?** No. Surfshark is part of Nord Security, a Lithuanian-Dutch cybersecurity group. The operational headquarters is in Amsterdam. There is no Chinese ownership or Chinese government involvement. The misconception spread through search suggestions and bad Reddit posts — the actual corporate structure is publicly documented. **What are the downsides of Surfshark?** The three real downsides are: Nord Security corporate-parent relationship (same group as NordVPN), weaker upload speed retention compared to download, and a meaningful price jump at renewal after the introductory 2-year term. None of these are dealbreakers for most users, but all three are worth knowing before you sign up. **Is NordVPN better than Surfshark?** NordVPN wins on raw speed, server count, and audit depth. Surfshark wins on price and unlimited devices. They share a corporate parent since 2022, so the "two horse race" framing is dated — both products are run by the same group but with separate teams and separate audits. Choose NordVPN if you want the deeper security posture and top-tier speed, Surfshark if you want the cheapest audited VPN with unlimited connections. **Does Surfshark work with Netflix?** Yes. We confirmed access to Netflix US, UK, Canada, Japan, Germany, France, and several other regional libraries in April 2026. If a specific server shows the "proxy detected" error, rotate to a different server in the same country — that almost always resolves it. Netflix's tighter enforcement on ad-supported plans and live events affects all VPN providers, not just Surfshark. **How many devices can I use with one Surfshark account?** Unlimited. One subscription covers every laptop, phone, tablet, TV, games console, and router in the household with no device cap. This is the strongest device policy of any premium VPN in 2026. **What is the cheapest way to buy Surfshark?** The 2-year Starter plan at $1.99/month ($53.73 total for 24 months plus 3 bonus months) is the cheapest tier. All 2-year plans include 3 free bonus months and a 30-day money-back guarantee. Renewal prices are higher than the intro rate. **Can I cancel Surfshark and get a refund?** Yes. Every plan includes a 30-day money-back guarantee. iOS, Android, and macOS app store purchases also include a 7-day free trial. Refunds are handled through the account dashboard or live chat support. ---
📖 Keep Reading: Still deciding? Our Best VPNs of 2026 guide ranks the top 10 VPNs by speed, security, and value. For the head-to-head, jump to Surfshark vs NordVPN.
## How Surfshark Compares
  • Surfshark vs NordVPN — two products, same corporate parent — which one is actually worth your money
  • Surfshark vs ExpressVPN — unlimited devices at $1.99/mo versus BVI jurisdiction and 23 audits at $3.49/mo
  • Surfshark vs Proton VPN — budget unlimited-device VPN versus the privacy-first Swiss alternative
